title.zh: 预告刀兵的白耳白喙小兽
title.en: White-Eared, White-Muzzled Beast That Portends War

summary.zh: 倚帝山小兽，形如鼣鼠，白耳白喙，它一现身，所在之国就要兴大兵，是中次十一经灾兆群兽之一。
summary.en: A small beast of Mount Yidi shaped like a fei-rat, with white ears and a white muzzle; wherever it appears, that state will raise great armies.

description.zh:
《中次十一经》记倚帝之山“其上多玉，其下多金”，山中有兽“状如鼣鼠，白耳白喙，名曰狙如，见则其国有大兵”。鼣鼠是《尔雅·释兽》所列鼠类之一，具体何指，汉晋以来的注家已经说不清楚；可以确定的只是狙如生得像一只鼠，耳朵和嘴喙都是白色。

兆示的分量与体形全不相称：一只白耳小鼠现身，应验的却是举国刀兵。《中次十一经》一带的山里群集着这类报灾的鸟兽：丰山的雍和状如猿而赤目赤喙，见则国有大恐；复州之山的跂踵状如鸮而一足彘尾，见则其国大疫。恐慌、瘟疫、战争各有信使，司兵的狙如是其中最不起眼的一头。
description.en:
Mount Yidi carries jade above and gold below. Its beast, the Juru, is shaped like a fei-rat — one of the rodents listed in the Erya glossary, which commentators since Han times have failed to identify with any certainty — with white ears and a white muzzle. The formula of consequence stands out of all proportion to the animal: wherever it appears, that state will see great armies raised.

The eleventh central sequence is thick with such omen beasts. On Mount Feng the Yonghe, an ape-like creature with red eyes and a red muzzle, portends great panic; on Mount Fuzhou the Qizhong, an owl-shaped bird with a single foot and a pig's tail, portends plague. Panic, pestilence and war each have their courier in these hills, and the little white-eared herald of war is the least conspicuous of them all.

value.en: Like a fei-rat with white ears and muzzle

image.sourceTitle: Imperial Encyclopaedia - Animal Kingdom - pic309 - 狙如圖
